name: North Carolina
tagline: A record-breaking AI data-center boom, a fresh AI executive order, and a first-ever statewide cybersecurity plan — all moving forward despite a still-unfinished state budget.

execSummary.summary: North Carolina is one of the hottest AI-infrastructure markets in the country, but it is buying technology through an unusual political moment. The structural story is opportunity: Amazon's $10B Richmond County campus is the largest single capital project in state history, and Apple, Microsoft, Meta and Google are all expanding data centers in the state. Governor Josh Stein's September 2025 Executive Order 24 stood up an AI Leadership Council, an AI Accelerator inside NCDIT, and agency AI oversight teams, while NCDIT published the state's first five-year cybersecurity strategic plan (2025-2030) and doubled its security program. The complication: the General Assembly failed to enact a full FY2025-27 budget — North Carolina was the only state to end 2025 without one — and has been operating on mini-budgets. For Dell, demand and policy tailwinds are strong, but full-budget uncertainty means timing discipline matters. The buying motion runs through NCDIT Statewide IT Contracts, where RFP cycles have been cut from ~8 months to ~8 weeks.
opportunities: AI-infrastructure pull-through tied to the nation-leading data-center boom (PowerEdge, networking, services) and UNC-system research computing.
Governed, on-prem and private-cloud AI for agencies operationalizing Executive Order 24 and the NCDIT AI Accelerator pilots.
Whole-of-state cybersecurity buildout under the new 2025-2030 plan and a doubled NCDIT security program — secure compute, infrastructure, modernization.
Endpoint, client and lifecycle refresh across cabinet agencies and a faster NCDIT procurement pipeline (8-week RFPs).

stats.label: AWS Richmond County campus
stats.note: Largest capital project in state history (June 2025)

stats.label: Executive Order 24 on AI
stats.note: AI Council + AI Accelerator in NCDIT

stats.label: FY2025-26 recommended budget
stats.note: Stein recommended; full budget not enacted

budget.intro: North Carolina budgets on a two-year (biennial) cycle, but the FY2025-27 cycle is atypical: the House and Senate deadlocked over income-tax cuts and never enacted a full budget, leaving the state operating on a series of 'mini-budgets.' Governor Stein's recommended budget was roughly $33.6B for FY2025-26 and $34.3B for FY2026-27. There is no single statewide 'IT budget' line; technology spend is distributed across agencies and a set of named technology, AI, cybersecurity and broadband programs.

stats.label: Full budgets enacted 2025
stats.note: Only state to end 2025 without one

notes: Budget figures are from the Governor's recommended budget; a full enacted FY2025-27 budget did not pass — confirm appropriations status before forecasting.
Mixed units: BEAD/AWS figures are program/investment totals, not annual operating appropriations.
Federal funds (BEAD, ARPA, SLCGP cyber grants) are a meaningful share of technology-adjacent dollars.

initiatives.name: AI Data-Center Boom
initiatives.summary: Amazon's $10B Richmond County campus (20 buildings, ~800 acres) is the largest capital project in state history, and Apple ($175M Maiden expansion), Microsoft (Catawba County), Meta (~$750M across three sites) and Google (Charlotte) are all expanding. Duke Energy projects demand growing eight times faster over 15 years. This is the strongest tailwind for Dell AI-infrastructure and ISG demand in the state.

initiatives.name: Executive Order 24 — Trustworthy AI
initiatives.summary: Stein's EO 24 created a 25-member AI Leadership Council (co-chaired by the IT and Commerce secretaries), an AI Accelerator inside NCDIT as the state's central AI hub, and agency AI oversight teams. Each cabinet agency must propose at least three AI use cases within 180 days. NC's AI standing reportedly jumped from 36th to 7th nationally — an opening for governed, auditable, on-prem AI on sensitive data.

initiatives.name: Statewide Cybersecurity Plan 2025-2030
initiatives.summary: NCDIT published North Carolina's first five-year cybersecurity strategic plan, anchored by six goals including zero-trust architecture, data classification and statewide collaboration. The department doubled its cybersecurity and risk-management program under a new statewide CISO and uses a whole-of-state model extending protection to local government — a broad surface for secure compute, infrastructure and managed services.

initiatives.name: $1.5B BEAD Broadband Program
initiatives.summary: NCDIT administers North Carolina's ~$1.53B federal BEAD allocation; the state has been approved to award $300M+ to connect 93,000+ locations and targets 210,000 additional connections by end of 2026. Primarily fiber deployment, but the connectivity expansion enlarges the addressable base of connected endpoints and edge sites across rural North Carolina.

procurement.intro: The N.C. Department of Information Technology (NCDIT) is the central technology procurement authority. Its Statewide IT Procurement Office develops and manages Statewide IT Contracts available to state agencies, local government and educational entities, and sets standards for IT products and services. Non-IT goods run through the Department of Administration's Division of Purchase & Contract. NCDIT has sharply compressed RFP cycles — from roughly eight months to eight weeks.

vehicles.name: NCDIT Statewide IT Contracts
vehicles.detail: Primary vehicle. Pre-established contracts open to state agencies, local gov and education. Confirm Dell agreements and resellers are current and correctly scoped.

vehicles.name: DOA Statewide Term Contracts
vehicles.detail: Non-IT goods and services through the Division of Purchase & Contract; IT items are carved out to NCDIT.

vehicles.name: Cooperative / federal pathways
vehicles.detail: Local entities and federally funded programs (BEAD, SLCGP cyber grants) bring additional buying pathways alongside statewide contracts.

entities.name: NCDIT
entities.role: Central IT procurement, policy, AI Accelerator, cybersecurity and broadband — leads the statewide technology motion.

entities.name: Cabinet agencies (HHS, DPS, DMV/Transportation)
entities.role: Large agency IT spenders; each now runs an AI oversight team under EO 24.

entities.name: UNC System & research universities
entities.role: Major independent research-computing and AI-infrastructure buyers (NC State, UNC-Chapel Hill, Research Triangle).

entities.name: Local government
entities.role: Reached through the whole-of-state cyber model and cooperative purchasing.

procurement.competition: North Carolina is a competitive, contract-driven market with incumbents across compute, networking and services. Differentiation comes from AI-infrastructure capability tied to the data-center boom, governed AI for EO 24 workloads, and whole-of-state security offerings — plus moving at the pace of NCDIT's faster RFP cycles.

powerStructure.name: Josh Stein
powerStructure.title: Governor
powerStructure.note: Signed Executive Order 24 on AI; champions the data-center / AI-infrastructure agenda and has pushed for a full budget.

powerStructure.name: Nate Denny
powerStructure.title: Secretary of IT & State CIO
powerStructure.note: Senate-confirmed May 2026; sworn in April 2026. Two decades in NC public-sector IT; previously led the state's $500M+ broadband expansion.

powerStructure.name: Bernice Russell-Bond
powerStructure.title: Statewide CISO
powerStructure.note: Leads the doubled cybersecurity program and the 2025-2030 strategic plan; partners with the Carolina Cyber Network.

powerStructure.name: AI Leadership Council
powerStructure.title: AI governance body
powerStructure.note: 25 members co-chaired by the IT and Commerce secretaries; initial deliverables due June 30, 2026.

powerStructure.name: N.C. General Assembly
powerStructure.title: Appropriations
powerStructure.note: House/Senate deadlock over tax cuts left the FY2025-27 full budget unenacted — the key variable for spend timing.

news.title: Senate confirms Nate Denny as NCDIT Secretary & State CIO
news.impact: Tech leadership stabilizes under a broadband-experienced insider after Piccione's departure.

news.title: Stein announces broadband projects to connect 93,000 locations
news.impact: BEAD dollars begin flowing; rural connectivity and connected-endpoint base expand.

news.title: NCDIT recaps a landmark 2025 in AI, cyber and connectivity
news.impact: AI rank 36th to 7th; cyber program doubled; RFP cycles cut ~76% — a faster, AI-forward buyer.

news.title: North Carolina launches AI Leadership Council
news.impact: Governance scaffolding for agency AI adoption stands up; demand for governed AI grows.

news.title: Governor Stein signs Executive Order 24 on trustworthy AI
news.impact: AI Accelerator and agency oversight teams created; agencies must surface AI use cases.

news.title: Amazon to invest $10B in Richmond County AI data centers
news.impact: Largest capital project in state history; anchors NC as a top AI-infrastructure market.

dellActions: Lead the AI-infrastructure conversation: tie Dell ISG / AI Factory offerings to the record data-center boom and UNC-system research-computing buyers.
Engage NCDIT's AI Accelerator and agency AI oversight teams early — position governed, private/on-prem AI for sensitive EO 24 workloads.
Map to the 2025-2030 cybersecurity plan and whole-of-state model — secure compute, zero-trust infrastructure, and managed services across state and local.
Validate that Dell's NCDIT Statewide IT Contract coverage and resellers are current and scoped for target agencies; move at the pace of 8-week RFP cycles.
Watch the full-budget situation — sequence proposals around mini-budget timing and confirm appropriations before forecasting large agency deals.
